back_translation: The wife of one of the sons of the prophets cried out to Elisha, “Your servant my husband is dead, and you know that your servant feared the LORD. Now the creditor is coming to take my two children as slaves.”
Elisha asked her, “How can I help you? Tell me, what do you have in the house?” She said, “Your maidservant has nothing in the house except a single jar of oil.”
Elisha said, “Go, borrow empty vessels from all your neighbors; do not ask for only a few.
Then go in, shut the door behind you and your sons, and pour oil into all these vessels, setting aside each one as it is filled.”
So she left him, shut the door behind her and her sons; they kept bringing her the vessels, and she kept pouring.
When the vessels were full, she said to her son, “Bring me another vessel.” But he said, “There are no more.” Then the oil stopped.
She went and told the man of God, and he said, “Go, sell the oil and pay your debt; you and your sons can live on the rest.”
One day Elisha traveled to Shunem, where a prominent woman urged him to eat a meal. So whenever he passed by, he would stop there to eat.
She said to her husband, “Behold, I know that the man who regularly passes our way is a holy man of God.
Let us make a small room on the roof and put a bed, a table, a chair, and a lamp in it for him, so that when he comes to us he can stay there.”
One day Elisha came there, went up to the upper room, and lay down.
He said to Gehazi his servant, “Call this Shunammite woman.” When Gehazi had called her, she stood before him.

Elisha said to Gehazi, “Tell her, ‘Behold, you have gone to all this trouble for us. What can we do for you? Shall we speak for you to the king or the commander of the army?’” She answered, “I dwell content among my own people.”
Elisha asked, “Then what can be done for her?” Gehazi answered, “She has no son, and her husband is old.”
Elisha said, “Call her.” So Gehazi called her, and she stood at the doorway.
Elisha said, “At this time next year you will embrace a son.” She said, “No, my lord; do not deceive your maidservant, O man of God.”
But the woman conceived, and at that time the next year she bore a son, just as Elisha had told her.
When the child grew up, one day he went out to his father, who was with the harvesters.
He cried to his father, “My head! My head!” So the father told a servant, “Carry him to his mother.”
The servant carried him to his mother; the boy sat on her lap until noon, and then he died.
She went up and laid him on the bed of the man of God, shut the door, and went out.
She called her husband and said, “Please send me one of the servants and one of the donkeys, that I may hurry to the man of God and return.”
He asked, “Why go to him today? It is not a New Moon or a Sabbath.” She said, “Everything is all right.”
Then she saddled the donkey and told her servant, “Drive on; do not slow down for me unless I tell you.”
So she set out and went to the man of God at Mount Carmel. When the man of God saw her at a distance, he said to Gehazi his servant, “Behold, there is the Shunammite woman.
Run now to meet her and ask, ‘Are you all right? Is your husband all right? Is your child all right?’” She answered, “Everything is all right.”

When she reached the man of God at the mountain, she clung to his feet. Gehazi came to push her away, but the man of God said, “Leave her alone, for her soul is in deep distress, and the LORD has hidden it from me and has not told me.”
She said, “Did I ask my lord for a son? Did I not say, ‘Do not deceive me’?”
Elisha said to Gehazi, “Gird your waist, take my staff in your hand, and go. If you meet anyone, do not greet him, and if anyone greets you, do not answer. Then lay my staff on the boy’s face.”
But the mother of the boy said, “As the LORD lives and as you yourself live, I will not leave you.” So Elisha got up and followed her.
Gehazi went on ahead and laid the staff on the boy’s face, but there was no sound or response. So he returned to meet Elisha and told him, “The boy has not awakened.”
When Elisha came to the house, the boy was lying dead on his bed.
He went in, shut the door behind the two of them, and prayed to the LORD.
Then Elisha got up and lay on the boy, putting his mouth on his mouth, his eyes on his eyes, his hands on his hands; and as he stretched himself over him, the boy’s flesh became warm.
Elisha got up and walked back and forth in the room, then went up and stretched himself over him again; and the boy sneezed seven times and opened his eyes.
Elisha called Gehazi and said, “Call this Shunammite woman.” So he called her, and when she came, Elisha said, “Pick up your son.”
She came in, fell at his feet, bowed to the ground, then picked up her son and went out.

Elisha returned to Gilgal while there was a famine in the land. As the sons of the prophets were sitting before him, he said to his servant, “Set on the large pot and boil stew for the sons of the prophets.”

One of them went out to the field to gather herbs and found a wild vine, from which he gathered wild gourds to fill his garment; he came back and cut them into the pot of stew, though no one knew what they were.
They poured it out for the men to eat, but when they tasted the stew they cried out, “There is death in the pot, O man of God!” And they could not eat it.
Elisha said, “Bring flour.” He threw it into the pot and said, “Pour it out for the people to eat.” And there was nothing harmful in the pot.
A man came from Baal-shalishah bringing the man of God bread of the firstfruits—twenty loaves of barley bread and fresh ears of grain in his sack. Elisha said, “Give it to the people to eat.”
His servant asked, “How can I set this before a hundred men?” But Elisha said, “Give it to the people to eat, for thus says the LORD: ‘They will eat and have some left over.’”
So he set it before them, and they ate and had some left over, according to the word of the LORD.
